# Te Reo Māori translation — open call for native-speaker review

> **Status:** translation drafted, pending review before wider distribution.
> **Pages:** [`index-mi.html`](../index-mi.html), [`partners-mi.html`](../partners-mi.html)
> **PR:** [#7](https://github.com/robertmccallnz/ai-warrior/pull/7)
> **PDF:** [`docs/review-invitation.pdf`](../docs/review-invitation.pdf) — printable bilingual version for sharing with reviewers offline.

---

## English

Tēnā koutou.

The AI Warrior course at The Kiwi Dialectic has been translated into te reo Māori. The kaupapa is to make working-class AI literacy reachable in te reo, alongside the existing English version, and to keep both pages in step as the course grows.

This is a first draft by a non-native speaker, written with care but with limits. Before we merge for wider distribution, we are explicitly inviting native speakers, kaiako reo, and iwi-connected reviewers to read the translation and tell us where the language can be sharper, kinder, or more faithful to the voices the project tries to stand alongside.

We would especially appreciate guidance on phrases where English carries political and cultural weight that does not transfer cleanly into te reo, including:

- **"indigenous-led"** — currently rendered with phrasings like *"e ārahina ana e ngā iwi taketake"*. Is *iwi taketake* the right framing in every context, or do specific sections call for *mana whenua*, *tangata whenua*, *mātāwaka*, or another term?
- **"consent-led protocols"** — currently rendered as *"tikanga ārahi-whakaae"*. Is there a more grounded te ao Māori framing (e.g. tied to *manaakitanga*, *kaitiakitanga*, *tūhono whakaae*) that better reflects how consent is actually held by iwi and hapū?
- **"data sovereignty"** — currently *"mana motuhake raraunga"*. Does this read as natural alongside Māori Data Sovereignty Network and Te Mana Raraunga conventions, or should we adopt their preferred wording verbatim?
- **"peak body"**, **"working-class"**, **"pedagogy circles"** — translated functionally; open to better idiomatic choices.
- **Section nav** — *Ngā Pou*, *Ngā Taputapu*, *Ngā Kaiwhakaaro*, *Ngā Hoa*, *Pouaka Pāpori*, *Pouaka Pāpāho*. Flag any that read as forced or non-native.
- **Tagline** — *"Whakangungua te hinengaro. Whakangaihia te iwi."* (kept consistent with the existing Hotere MI poster). Confirm or improve.
- **Quotes** — Gramsci, Kropotkin, Graeber, Freire, Deleuze, Bakunin are paraphrased rather than transliterated. Reviewers welcome to suggest better renderings.

The goal is a final version that is authentic, not performative — language that reflects the iwi voices the AI Warrior project tries to walk alongside, and that an iwi reader recognises as theirs.

**How to contribute**

- Open an issue on this repo with `[reo review]` in the title, quoting the line(s) you want to revise, or
- Comment directly on [PR #7](https://github.com/robertmccallnz/ai-warrior/pull/7), or
- Email **hello@kiwidialectic.com** if you prefer not to use GitHub.

All suggestions will be credited (with permission) in a `CONTRIBUTORS-REO.md` file before merge. Ngā mihi nui ki ngā kaiarotake katoa.

---

## Te Reo Māori

Tēnā koutou katoa.

Kua whakamāoritia te akoranga Hei Toa AI a Te Kiwi Dialectic ki te reo Māori. Ko te kaupapa, kia tae te mātauranga AI nā te hunga mahi ki te reo Māori, ki te taha o te putanga reo Pākehā kei te tū kē, ā, kia haere ngātahi ngā whārangi e rua i te tipu haere o te akoranga.

He tuhinga tuatahi tēnei nā tētahi ehara nei i te kaikōrero taketake. He āta whakaaro tōna tuhinga, engari he iti tonu ōna mātauranga. I mua i te whakakotahitanga mō te tohatoha whānui, e karangahia ana ngā kaikōrero taketake, ngā kaiako reo, me ngā kaiarotake e hono ana ki ngā iwi, kia pānui i te whakamāoritanga, kia tohutohu mai ki hea ka taea te whakakoi ake, te whakaatawhai ake, te whakapono ake ki ngā reo e whakanui nei mātou.

Ka nui te mihi ki ngā kupu āwhina mō ēnei kupu Pākehā e mau ana i te taumahatanga ā-tōrangapū, ā-ahurea hoki — kāore e ngāwari te whakawhiti ki te reo:

- **"indigenous-led"** — kei te whakamāoritia ki te kīanga *"e ārahina ana e ngā iwi taketake"*. He tika rānei *iwi taketake* i ngā wāhi katoa, me whakakapi rānei ki *mana whenua*, *tangata whenua*, *mātāwaka*, ki tētahi atu kupu rānei i ētahi wāhanga?
- **"consent-led protocols"** — kei te whakamāoritia ki *"tikanga ārahi-whakaae"*. Tērā pea he kīanga e pai ake ana i te ao Māori (hei tauira, e tūhono ana ki te *manaakitanga*, te *kaitiakitanga*, te *tūhono whakaae*) hei whakaata i te āhua o te whakaae i puritia ai e te iwi me te hapū?
- **"data sovereignty"** — *"mana motuhake raraunga"*. He māori rānei tēnei kīanga i te taha o ngā tikanga a Te Mana Raraunga me te Māori Data Sovereignty Network, me whai tonu rānei i ā rātou kīanga tūturu?
- **"peak body"**, **"working-class"**, **"pedagogy circles"** — kua whakamāoritia hāngai noa; e wātea ana ki ngā kīanga pai ake.
- **Ngā kupu whakataki o ngā wāhanga** — *Ngā Pou*, *Ngā Taputapu*, *Ngā Kaiwhakaaro*, *Ngā Hoa*, *Pouaka Pāpori*, *Pouaka Pāpāho*. Tohua mai mehemea kāore i te māori, kāore rānei i te rite ki te reo o te kaikōrero taketake.
- **Te kupu pou** — *"Whakangungua te hinengaro. Whakangaihia te iwi."* (kua noho tahi ki te pānui Hotere MI o nāianei). Whakaaehia, whakapaihia rānei.
- **Ngā whakatauākī** — ko ngā kupu a Gramsci, Kropotkin, Graeber, Freire, Deleuze, Bakunin, kua whakamāoritia mā te whakaaro, kaua mā te kupu-ki-te-kupu. E wātea ana ngā kaiarotake ki te whakahou.

Ko te whāinga, he putanga whakamutunga he pono, ehara i te whakaatu noa — he reo e whakaata ana i ngā reo o ngā iwi e whai nei te kaupapa Hei Toa AI ki te haere tahi, ā, e mōhio ai te kaipānui Māori, nō rātou tēnei.

**Me pēhea te tuku kupu āwhina**

- Whakatuwheratia he take ki tēnei pātaka me te *[reo review]* i te taitara, me te whakapuaki i ngā rārangi e hiahia ana koe ki te whakahou, ki
- Tāpiri kōrero ki [PR #7](https://github.com/robertmccallnz/ai-warrior/pull/7), ki
- Tukuna he īmēra ki **hello@kiwidialectic.com** mēnā kāore koe e hiahia ki te whakamahi i GitHub.

Ka tuhia ngā ingoa o ngā kaiarotake katoa (me te whakaae) ki te kōnae `CONTRIBUTORS-REO.md` i mua i te whakakotahitanga. Ngā mihi nui ki a koutou katoa.

---

*Robert McCall · Te Kiwi Dialectic · Ōtepoti / Dunedin*
